text:
MARKET PULSE — Today's tape offered a modest relief bounce — SPY +0.6%, QQQ +0.7%, IWM +1.2% — but don't mistake a dead-cat bid for a trend change. The 20-day damage is severe across the board: SPY -5.0%, IWM -4.7%, RSP -5.3%. Fear & Greed sits at 51.5, deceptively neutral given that only 1 of 11 sectors holds positive 20-day momentum. Regime is labeled Neutral but the tape is still in distribution.

MACRO BACKDROP — Fed Funds at 3.64% with CPI at 2.7% leaves real rates modestly positive; the Fed is on hold. Core PCE is ticking up (+0.05 change), flagged hawkish — that's the number Powell watches most. NFP momentum is softening (dovish, -0.22 change) and consumer sentiment is deteriorating. Brent crude's -14.63 change is disinflationary but also a growth warning. Yield curve is flat-to-neutral; no inversion signal, but no green light either. The setup is stagflation-lite: slowing growth, sticky core inflation, Fed paralyzed.

LEADERS & LAGGARDS — Energy is the only game in town: XLE +10.4% over 20 days, Energy 7 basket +13.1% (YTD +32.7%), with DVN +18.9%, FANG +17.5%, COP +17.2%, EOG +16.9% leading individually. Optical Comms theme is exceptional at +33.2% over 20 days. Semis bounced hard today (+3.6%), MRVL +21.7% over 20 days. On the other side, Old Metals are getting destroyed — GOLD -23.2%, SCCO -23.1%, WPM -22.1%, NEM -18.5% — despite GLD's +3.0% single-day bounce. XLP -8.4%, XLRE -7.3%, XLV -7.3% over 20 days are dragging defensives. Housing basket -12.0% over 20 days; not a shelter trade right now.

RISK SIGNALS — VIX at 25.3 (52-week range 13.5-52.3) remains elevated, in the 70th percentile of its risk-scoring band. HYG -1.4% vs TLT -3.1% — credit and duration both under pressure simultaneously, a toxic combo. GLD's 20-day reading of -12.1% despite today's bounce signals forced selling and possible margin-call liquidation, not fundamental weakness. IJK and IJS volume spikes (16.9x and 12.8x average) suggest significant style-rotation positioning, likely institutional rebalancing into mid-cap value. RSP underperforming SPY at -5.3% vs -5.0% over 20 days confirms breadth is not recovering.

WHAT TO WATCH — XLE: holds 20-day leadership; any crack below recent support invalidates the only working sector thesis. MRVL: Semi 7 momentum returning, watch for follow-through above 20-day resistance. GLD: today's +3.0% bounce after -12.1% drawdown — is this a base or a dead-cat? Watch the $270 area. IJK/IJS: the 16.9x/12.8x volume spike in mid-cap value demands follow-through tomorrow to confirm rotation is real. Core PCE release this week — any upside surprise re-prices Fed cuts and pressures QQQ hard.
